name: BREEZCIRC — PORTABLE NECK FAN
title: BREEZCIRC
subtitle: Portable Cooling Neck Fan with Reusable Gel-Cooling Technology
summary: Lightweight neck fan integrating reusable gel-cooling technology to pre-cool incoming airflow, improving user comfort in extreme heat environments. Led a 6-member team to secure a $5,000 development grant.
description: Conventional neck fans circulate ambient hot air, providing limited cooling in extreme heat. BreezeCirc addresses this by integrating reusable gel-cooling technology that pre-cools incoming airflow, improving user comfort and reducing heat-related health risks.

Led a 6-member team through full prototype development from concept to physical assembly. Designed the mechanical form and ergonomics in SolidWorks, creating modular snap-fit components with detachable gel compartments for easy maintenance, and 3D printed and assembled prototypes to verify fit and structural integrity.
keyOutcomes: Developed a modular snap-fit prototype enabling easy assembly and component replacement.
Project secured a $5,000 development grant to advance BreezeCirc into a fully functional prototype.
Strengthened early skills in leadership, CAD modelling, and additive manufacturing.

name: SORTIFY — PARCEL SORTING SYSTEM
title: SORTIFY
subtitle: Automated Parcel Size Sorting System for Efficient Logistics Handling
summary: Automated parcel sorting system using time-of-flight sensors to measure dimensions and PLC-controlled pneumatic actuators to route parcels to designated bins. Designed and wired all electrical systems including PLC, Arduino, and conveyor belt.
description: E-commerce logistics demand faster, accurate, and gentle parcel handling. Sortify automates parcel sorting by size, using time-of-flight sensors to measure dimensions and PLC-controlled pneumatic actuators to direct parcels to bins, while ensuring safety and package protection.

Initiated the system concept and led a 5-member multidisciplinary team. Designed and wired all electrical systems including PLC, Arduino, and conveyor belt integration. Programmed PLC ladder logic and Arduino firmware to automate detection and sorting, and integrated safety features including emergency stop and system interlocks.
keyOutcomes: Developed a fully functional prototype capable of automated parcel sorting.
Secured hands-on experience in PLC programming, Arduino integration, sensors, and pneumatics.
Gained leadership experience managing a 5-member multidisciplinary team.
Enhanced problem-solving skills through iterative testing and calibration.

name: FORMIFY — VACUUM FORMER
title: FORMIFY
subtitle: Custom Vacuum Forming Machine for Lightweight Aerodynamic Car Components
summary: Large-scale vacuum forming machine enabling the Monash Shell Eco-Marathon Team to self-manufacture lightweight windshields and aerodynamic components, delivered within a $1,000 budget. Responsible for PID control design and all electrical wiring.
description: Producing custom lightweight plastic components for aerodynamic vehicles is costly and time-consuming. Formify was developed as a large-scale vacuum forming machine to enable the Monash Shell Eco-Marathon Team to self-manufacture windshields and body components efficiently.

Designed and wired the PID controller and control panel for precise temperature regulation of industrial heating elements. Performed laser cutting on vacuum bed panels to optimise airflow distribution. Collaborated with SEM stakeholders to understand performance requirements and assisted in system assembly and troubleshooting under tight resource constraints.
keyOutcomes: Formed plastic parts to target size and depth (~0.5 m) within a $1,000 budget.
Enabled the SEM team to self-produce lightweight components, reducing reliance on external suppliers.
Gained hands-on experience in PID control, industrial heating, vacuum mechanics, and electrical wiring.

name: FPGA VIDEO PROCESSING SYSTEM
title: FPGA VIDEO PROCESSOR
subtitle: Real-Time Hardware-Accelerated Image Processing on DE10-Lite FPGA with ESP32-CAM
summary: Real-time embedded image processing system on a DE10-Lite FPGA, capturing live video from ESP32-CAM and applying hardware-accelerated effects with interactive controls via accelerometer and gyroscope. Achieved 30 FPS and 52x speedup in edge detection.
description: Real-time video processing on embedded systems is challenging due to limited FPGA resources and strict timing constraints. This project implements a hardware-accelerated image processing pipeline supporting flip, Gaussian blur, and Sobel edge detection with single and quad display modes.

Developed a multiprocessor framework using 3 Nios II processors for concurrent operation: ProcA handling image processing, ProcB managing SPI communication and accelerometer/gyroscope input, and ProcC driving VGA rendering. Implemented display mode switching, gesture controls, and optimised processor concurrency to maximise FPS.
keyOutcomes: Achieved 30 FPS in full RGB mode on resource-constrained FPGA hardware.
Implemented hardware-accelerated filtering with up to 52x speedup in edge detection.
Successfully demonstrated real-time hardware-software integration with motion sensor interactivity.
Gained deep skills in FPGA multiprocessor synchronization and real-time system design.

name: PENGUINPI — AUTONOMOUS ROBOT
title: PENGUINPI
subtitle: Fully Autonomous Retail Assistant Robot with SLAM Mapping and YOLO Detection
summary: Fully autonomous robot that maps its environment using SLAM, detects fruits with YOLO, and navigates safely via A* path planning with obstacle avoidance. Scored second-highest in live demonstration with near-perfect fruit collection.
description: Autonomous retail robots must navigate cluttered environments safely while detecting and interacting with objects in real time. PenguinPi simulates a retail assistant by performing SLAM-based mapping, YOLO-based fruit detection, and obstacle-aware navigation, integrating perception, mapping, and planning into a single autonomous system.

Implemented fruit mapping using DBSCAN clustering and designed an A* path planner with obstacle-penalty cost for safe navigation. Developed front-obstacle detection and recovery behaviour including reverse and 360 degree scan. Trained and deployed YOLO for real-time inference and integrated perception, mapping, and planning into a unified autonomous workflow.
keyOutcomes: Achieved fully autonomous mapping and navigation in a continuous task.
Robot navigated accurately with obstacle-aware paths and reliable fruit detection.
Scored second-highest in live demo — perfect fruit collection, minor light collision.
Gained hands-on skills in autonomous navigation, SLAM, and robotics perception-planning pipelines.

name: ROBOT DOG — VOICE AI CONTROL
title: ROBOT DOG RESKIN
subtitle: Unitree Go1 Robot Dog with Friendly Design Features and Voice AI Control
summary: Enhanced a Unitree Go1 robot dog with friendly physical features and voice AI control for Monash Automation school outreach demonstrations. Designed and 3D-printed custom mounts and a ball-joint tail, and developed ChatGPT-powered voice command control.
description: As part of Monash Automation's outreach initiative, this project aimed to make the Unitree Go1 robot dog more engaging and approachable for school demonstrations. The robot was enhanced with friendly physical features and voice AI control, enabling natural speech commands for younger audiences.

Designed and 3D-printed a custom Raspberry Pi mount for seamless AI hardware integration. Developed voice command functionality using the ChatGPT API for natural language control. CAD-designed and 3D-printed a ball-and-joint tail to enhance perceived friendliness, and assisted in all mechanical and electrical modifications to integrate the design with AI control.
keyOutcomes: Delivered a friendly, interactive Unitree Go1 robot dog for school demonstrations.
Enabled voice-controlled AI interactions, significantly improving audience engagement.
Gained practical experience in prototyping, CAD/3D printing, and AI integration.
